titleStmt.title: Memorandum of Conversation, by the Acting Secretary of State

head.hi: Memorandum of Conversation, by the Acting Secretary of State

div.p: The Minister of Finland called to see me this afternoon and left with me the aide-mémoire attached herewith.Not printed.

I told the Minister that this Government, as well as the people of the United States, continued to have the deepest interest in and sympathy for the people of Finland in their struggle to maintain their independence and their liberty. I said, however, that, for reasons which of course were obvious, this Government would have to continue observing developments and that it would not at this time be in a position to clarify its final attitude with regard to Finland beyond that already indicated. I said that it was my most earnest hope that the Government of Finland would not take any steps which would indicate an intention on the part of Finland to permit the liberties for which the Finnish people have so bravely fought to be now destroyed under the guise of German “cooperation”.

The Minister stated he was greatly concerned as to what the attitude of the British Government would be towards Finland. I said I had as yet no definite word with regard to that matter, but that I felt that for the time being the attitude of the British Government was more or less the attitude of this Government.
